---
title: "Spring phenology tier2 project"
subtitle: "Model performance figures (PEP725 data)"
author: "Constantin Zohner"
date: "Last run: `r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"

format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: default
    highlight-style: haddock
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  #warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
  output: true

always_allow_html: true
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<br>

### Driver names

-   Tmin: Minimum daily temperature
-   Tmax: Maximum daily temperature

<br>

# 1. Setup

<details>

<summary>Packages</summary>

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
require(data.table)
require(dplyr)
require(pbmcapply)
require(chillR)
require(zoo)
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Functions</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# Chilling and GDD calculation
#-------------------------------------------------------

# Chilling units
Chill.fun = function(Temp, threshold_low, threshold_up){
  if(between(Temp,threshold_low, threshold_up)) {CU=1} else {CU=0}
  return(CU)
}

# Forcing units
GDD.fun = function(Temp, threshold_low){
  if(Temp >= threshold_low) {GDD=Temp-threshold_low} else {GDD=0}
  return(GDD)
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Photoperiod model
#-------------------------------------------------------

# Function for photoperiod sensitivity with different k and p values
photoperiod_response <- function(DL, k, p) {
  DL_adjusted <- pmax(DL - 6, 0)  # Shift DL so that it starts at 6 hours instead of 0
  f_DL <- (log(1 + k * DL_adjusted) / log(1 + k * (20 - 6)))^p  # Normalize to DL = 20
  f_DL <- pmin(f_DL, 1)  # Cap at 1 from DL = 20 to 24
  return(f_DL)
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Chilling model
#-------------------------------------------------------

# Function for chilling dependency scaling factor
chilling_response <- function(Chilling, requirement) {
  factor <- pmin(Chilling / requirement, 1)  # chilling dependency reaches 1 at X chill days
  return(factor)
}
# Chilling = actual chilling unit 
# requirement = total chilling requirement (in days), i.e., after how many chill days is chilling completely fulfilled?


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Function to compute adjusted GDDs #
#-------------------------------------------------------

adjust_gdd <- function(GDD, Chilling, requirement=150, include_photo = F, DL = NULL, k = 1, p = 1) {
  f_Chill <- chilling_response(Chilling, requirement)
  
  if (include_photo) {
    if (is.null(DL)) stop("DL must be provided when include_photo is TRUE")
    f_Photo <- photoperiod_response(DL, k, p)
    GDD_adjusted <- GDD * f_Chill * f_Photo
  } else {
    GDD_adjusted <- GDD * f_Chill
  }
  
  return(GDD_adjusted)
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Replace NAs with neighbor values through interpolation
#-------------------------------------------------------

replace_na_with_neighbor_mean <- function(x) {
  if (!is.numeric(x)) return(x)
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(x)
  return(na.approx(x, na.rm = FALSE, rule = 2))
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Leave-one-out mean
#-------------------------------------------------------

# LOO mean that ignores NAs, returns NA if only one non-NA in group
loo_vec <- function(v){
  nn <- sum(!is.na(v)); ss <- sum(v, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (nn <= 1) return(rep(NA_real_, length(v)))
  res <- (ss - v) / (nn - 1)
  res[is.na(v)] <- ss/nn
  res
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Find date when GDD threshold is reached
#-------------------------------------------------------

# Return the 1-based index of the **first** element in `x` that is >= `thr`.
# If no such element exists (or inputs are empty/invalid), return NA_integer_.
# - `x` can contain NAs → they are ignored (do not match the condition).
# - Works for any numeric vector; no sorting assumption required.
first_ge <- function(x, thr) { 
  # Guard against empty x or undefined threshold
  if (length(x) == 0L || is.na(thr)) return(NA_integer_)
  
  # Which positions satisfy x >= thr? Take the first match.
  # If none match, `which(...)` is integer(0) and `[1]` yields NA.
  idx <- which(x >= thr)[1]
  
  # Normalize to an integer NA when there was no match
  if (is.na(idx)) NA_integer_ else idx
  }
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Directories</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# Define the base directory
#-------------------------------------------------

base.dir <- "/Users/goalie/Desktop/PhD/Publication_material/40_Spring_phenology_tier2/PEP725_Analysis/Analysis_input"


#-------------------------------------------------
# Build specific directories from the base
#-------------------------------------------------

# Climate
EOBS.dir = file.path(base.dir, "EOBS_data")

# Photoperiod
photo.dir = file.path(base.dir, "Photoperiod")

# Phenology
PEP.dir = file.path(base.dir, "PEP_drivers_final_spring/Merged_file")

# Output
PEP_drivers.dir = file.path(base.dir, "PEP_models")
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Load and prepare data</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# Phenology data
#-------------------------------------------------------

PEP.df <- fread(paste(PEP.dir, "pep_drivers_spring_data.csv", sep="/")) %>% 
  # select columns
  dplyr::select(c(s_id, species, timeseries, year, lat, lon, leaf_out,
                  GDD, GDD.chill.lo, GDD.chill.hi, GDD.chill.DL)) %>% 
  # Mean GDD sums for each timeseries
  group_by(s_id, species, timeseries) %>% 
  # keep series ≥20 years
  filter(n() >= 20) %>% 
  # Leave-one-out mean GDDs
  mutate(across(c(GDD, GDD.chill.lo, GDD.chill.hi, GDD.chill.DL),
                ~loo_vec(.x))) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  # create identifier columns
  mutate(site_year = paste0(s_id, "_", year),       # Identifier 1 (site x year combinations)
         ts_yr     = paste0(timeseries, "_", year)) # Identifier 2 (timeseries x year combinations)


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Photoperiod
#-------------------------------------------------------

photo.df = fread(paste(photo.dir, "Photoperiod.csv", sep="/"))


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Daily climatic datasets from EOBS
#-------------------------------------------------------

#define climate variables
list.files(EOBS.dir)
vn <- c("tn_ens_mean_0.1deg_reg_v30.0e.csv", # daily minimum temperature
        "tx_ens_mean_0.1deg_reg_v30.0e.csv") # daily maximum temperature

# Read into named list
DataList <- lapply(file.path(EOBS.dir, vn), fread)


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Merge data and add names to list
#-------------------------------------------------------

# Add additional datasets
DataList[[length(DataList) + 1]] <- photo.df
DataList[[length(DataList) + 1]] <- PEP.df

# Set all names
names(DataList) = c(vn, "photoperiod", "PEP")
names(DataList)

# remove individual tables
rm(photo.df, PEP.df)
```

</details>

<br>

# 2. Parallel computing

<details>

<summary>Create loop</summary>

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
###########################################################
# Loop through each observations using parallel computing #
###########################################################



# Define unique timeseries-year combinations
timeseries_year  = unique(DataList[["PEP"]]$ts_yr)

# Parallel processing function
parallelCalc <- function(timeseries_years, DataList) {
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # extract PEP data per timeseries x year combination
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  pheno.sub  <- DataList[["PEP"]][which(DataList[["PEP"]]$ts_yr==timeseries_years),]
  # Skip if doesn't exist
  if (nrow(pheno.sub) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Define the length of the period of interest in calendar units
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  year2     <- pheno.sub$year[1]
  year1     <- year2 - 1L
  start_doy <- sprintf("%d-01-01", year1)
  end_doy   <- sprintf("%d-06-30", year2)
  days      <- seq(as.Date(start_doy), as.Date(end_doy), by="days")
  
  #get number of days in year 1 and year 2
  days_year1 <- length(seq(as.Date(start_doy), as.Date(paste(year1,"-12-31", sep="")), by="days"))
  days_year2 <- length(seq(as.Date(paste0(year2,"-01-01")), as.Date(end_doy), by="days"))
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Load and merge all climate variables
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  # Function to load climate data
  get_subdata <- function(var_index) {
      # Year 1
      sub1 <- DataList[[var_index]][DataList[[var_index]]$s_id == pheno.sub$s_id &
                                      DataList[[var_index]]$year == year1, as.character(1:days_year1), with = FALSE]
      # Year 2
      sub2 <- DataList[[var_index]][DataList[[var_index]]$site_year == pheno.sub$site_year, as.character(1:days_year2), with = FALSE]
      if (nrow(sub1) == 0 || nrow(sub2) == 0) return(NULL)
      cbind(sub1, sub2)
    }
  
  # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Short Name        ; Long Name                               ; Unit [d-1]
  # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # TMIN              ; Minimum Air Temperature                 ; degC
  # TMAX              ; Maximum Air Temperature                 ; degC
  # ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  Tmin  <- get_subdata(1)
  Tmax  <- get_subdata(2)
  
  DL    <- cbind(
      DataList[["photoperiod"]][DataList[["photoperiod"]]$lat == pheno.sub$lat, as.character(1:days_year1), with = FALSE][1,],
      DataList[["photoperiod"]][DataList[["photoperiod"]]$lat == pheno.sub$lat, as.character(1:days_year2), with = FALSE][1,]
    )
  
 if (any(sapply(list(Tmin, Tmax, DL), is.null))) {
      return(NULL)
    }
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Table of climate variables
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
    
  daily_vals <- data.frame(
    Year  = lubridate::year(days),
    Month = lubridate::month(days),
    Day   = lubridate::day(days),
    Tmin  = as.numeric(unlist(Tmin, use.names = FALSE)),
    Tmax  = as.numeric(unlist(Tmax, use.names = FALSE)),
    DL    = as.numeric(unlist(DL, use.names = FALSE))) %>% 
    #replace NAs with neighbor mean
    mutate(across(c(Tmin, Tmax, DL), replace_na_with_neighbor_mean))
  
    
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Generate sub-dataframe to store results
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
    
  factors.sub <- pheno.sub %>% dplyr::select(s_id,species,timeseries,year,leaf_out)
    
    
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Shortest day of year
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  solstice = which(daily_vals$DL==min(daily_vals$DL, na.rm = T))[1] 
  if (is.na(solstice)) return(NULL)
  

  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Add chilling and GDD
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
    
  #Get hourly values
  hourly_vals = stack_hourly_temps(daily_vals, latitude=pheno.sub$lat)$hourtemps
    
  # Chilling and Forcing hours
  Chill_days = hourly_vals %>%
    #add Chilling and Forcing units
    mutate(Chill = sapply(Temp, Chill.fun, threshold_low=-10, threshold_up=10),
           GDD   = sapply(Temp, GDD.fun,   threshold_low=5)) %>%
    #summarise daytime hours
    group_by(Year, Month, Day) %>%
    summarise(Chill = mean(Chill, na.rm=T),
              GDD   = mean(GDD, na.rm=T))%>%
    ungroup() %>%
    #order
    dplyr::select(Chill, GDD) 
    
  # define November first as start date of chilling accumulation
  start_row <-  which(daily_vals$Month==11 & daily_vals$Day==1)  
    
  # Cbind
  daily_vals = cbind(daily_vals, Chill_days) %>%
    mutate(
      #continous count from Jan 1
      id = row_number(),
      # Sum chilling days from 1 November
      Chill_sum = ifelse(id < start_row, 0, cumsum(ifelse(id >= start_row, Chill, 0))),
      # Add chilling/photoperiod-adjusted GDDs
      GDD.chill.lo = adjust_gdd(GDD=GDD, Chilling=Chill_sum, requirement=75),
      GDD.chill.hi = adjust_gdd(GDD=GDD, Chilling=Chill_sum, requirement=150),
      GDD.chill.DL = adjust_gdd(GDD=GDD, Chilling=Chill_sum, requirement=150, include_photo = T, DL = DL)) %>% 
    #order
    dplyr::select(id, Year, Month, Day,
                  GDD, GDD.chill.lo, GDD.chill.hi, GDD.chill.DL) %>% 
    #calculate GDD sums from winter solstice
    filter(id >= solstice) %>% 
    mutate(GDD          = cumsum(GDD),
           GDD.chill.lo = cumsum(GDD.chill.lo),
           GDD.chill.hi = cumsum(GDD.chill.hi),
           GDD.chill.DL = cumsum(GDD.chill.DL)) %>% 
    #delete days of previous year
    filter(Year != as.integer(year1)) 
    

  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Predict leaf-out dates
  #-------------------------------------------------------------

  factors.sub = factors.sub %>% 
      mutate(leaf_out_GDD          = first_ge(daily_vals$GDD,          pheno.sub$GDD),
             leaf_out_GDD.chill.lo = first_ge(daily_vals$GDD.chill.lo, pheno.sub$GDD.chill.lo),
             leaf_out_GDD.chill.hi = first_ge(daily_vals$GDD.chill.hi, pheno.sub$GDD.chill.hi),
             leaf_out_GDD.chill.DL = first_ge(daily_vals$GDD.chill.DL, pheno.sub$GDD.chill.DL))
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  # Safe the table   
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  return(factors.sub)
    
}
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Run loop and safe data</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------------
# Run in parallel
#-------------------------------------------------------------

outputlist <- pbmclapply(timeseries_year, function(y) parallelCalc(y, DataList), mc.cores = 12, mc.preschedule = T)
climate.factors.df <- rbindlist(outputlist, fill=T)


#-------------------------------------------------------------
# Save results
#-------------------------------------------------------------

write.csv(climate.factors.df, paste(PEP_drivers.dir, "pep_model_predictions_LOO.csv", sep="/"))
```

</details>

<br>

# 3. Reproducibility

<details>

<summary>show</summary>

```{r}
# Datetime
Sys.time()

# Session info
sessionInfo()
```

</details>
